question: PBQ: Windows boot issue troubleshooting order
note: Start with symptoms and simple recovery options before destructive repair actions.
instructions: A Windows PC fails to boot correctly after an update. Put the troubleshooting steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Identify the exact boot error or symptom
Attempt Startup Repair or Safe Mode
Uninstall the recent update or driver if Safe Mode works
Run repair commands such as DISM and SFC if needed
Restore from a restore point or backup if repair fails
Verify normal boot and document the fix

question: PBQ: Windows symptom to tool matching
note: Use the tool that gives the most direct evidence for the symptom.
instructions: Match each troubleshooting symptom to the best Windows tool.

items.scenario: System is slow due to high disk usage
Unknown startup app launches at sign-in
Driver has a yellow warning icon
Need to check long-term performance counters
Need to disable services for clean boot testing

question: PBQ: Mobile app troubleshooting order
note: Mobile troubleshooting should start with simple user-safe fixes before reinstalling or resetting.
instructions: A mobile app crashes immediately after opening. Put the troubleshooting steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Confirm whether one app or multiple apps are affected
Force close the app and reopen it
Check for app and operating system updates
Clear app cache or storage if appropriate
Escalate or factory reset only if required and backed up

question: PBQ: Mobile symptom to likely cause
note: Mobile symptoms often map to connectivity, configuration, battery, sync, or application issues.
instructions: Match each mobile symptom to the most likely cause or setting to check.

items.scenario: Cellular data does not work after carrier change
Email no longer syncs on the phone
Battery drains quickly after installing an app
User cannot install apps because storage is full
Mobile browser keeps redirecting to ads

question: PBQ: Malware symptom identification
note: Symptoms alone do not prove malware, but they help determine the most likely investigation path.
instructions: Match each symptom to the most likely malware-related issue.

items.scenario: Search results redirect to unrelated websites
System is unusually slow after suspicious download

question: PBQ: Troubleshooting methodology order
note: This is the standard CompTIA troubleshooting flow.
instructions: Put the CompTIA troubleshooting methodology in the correct order.
correctOrder: Establish a theory of probable cause
Test the theory to determine the cause
Establish a plan of action and implement the solution
Verify full system functionality and implement preventive measures

question: PBQ: Network connectivity symptom matching
note: Use symptoms to separate local device, DNS, gateway, Wi-Fi, and service-specific problems.
instructions: Match each connectivity symptom to the first setting or area to check.

items.scenario: Can ping IP addresses but not domain names
No connectivity outside the local network
Only one Wi-Fi network has poor signal
VPN connects but internal apps are unreachable
Only one website fails while others work

question: PBQ: Application repair workflow
note: Preserve user data and gather evidence before reinstalling or escalating.
instructions: A business application opens but crashes during login. Put the troubleshooting steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Confirm the error message and affected users
Check whether the application service or website is available
Clear cache or repair the application if appropriate
Update or reinstall the application if needed
Verify login works and document the resolution

question: PBQ: OS recovery option selection
note: Choose the least destructive recovery option that solves the problem.
instructions: Match each recovery scenario to the best Windows recovery option.

items.scenario: Undo a recent system change without deleting personal files
Boot into troubleshooting tools when Windows will not start
Remove a bad update from recovery tools
Reinstall Windows with options to keep or remove files

question: PBQ: Printer software troubleshooting selection
note: Software-side printer issues often involve the queue, driver, default printer, or network path.
instructions: Match each printing symptom to the best first action.

items.scenario: Jobs are stuck and not printing
Network printer cannot be reached by name

question: PBQ: BSOD troubleshooting workflow
note: Blue screens are commonly caused by drivers, hardware, or corrupted system files.
instructions: Put the troubleshooting steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Boot into Safe Mode if necessary
Roll back or update the suspected driver

note: Choose the Windows recovery tool that best matches the symptom.
